messages.content:
You are an expert systems architect reviewing research for a 3-Layer Autonomous Survival System being added to an existing Rust/Axum Windows fleet management monorepo (8 gaming pods + server + external VPS). Review the research findings below and identify: (1) GAPS - what critical aspects were missed, (2) RISKS - what could go wrong that wasn't covered, (3) CORRECTIONS - factual errors or outdated assumptions, (4) IMPROVEMENTS - better approaches than proposed. Focus on: Windows service constraints, OpenRouter API reliability, cross-layer coordination, recovery system conflicts, binary integrity, and budget management. Be specific and actionable. Format: ## GAPS
- ...
## RISKS
- ...
## CORRECTIONS
- ...
## IMPROVEMENTS
- ...

Review this research for v31.0 Autonomous Survival System:

## STACK FINDINGS:
- Only 1 new crate needed: goblin 0.10 (PE header parsing). All others (sha2, reqwest::blocking, serde, toml) already in workspace.
- rc-watchdog has NO tokio runtime. Must use reqwest::blocking or create explicit Runtime::new() for async OpenRouter calls.
- Top 5 OpenRouter models: Qwen3 235B ($0.22/1M), DeepSeek R1 ($0.55/1M), DeepSeek V3 ($0.38/1M), Devstral 2 ($0.05/1M), Gemini 2.5 Pro ($1.25/1M).
- Per-pod child API keys via management key API for budget isolation.
- Cross-layer HTTP: watchdog POSTs directly to server /api/v1/pods/{id}/survival-report, bypassing dead rc-agent.

## ARCHITECTURE FINDINGS:
- Layer 1 (Smart Watchdog): extend existing rc-watchdog Windows service with smart_watchdog.rs + survival_state.rs modules. NOT a new binary.
- Layer 2 (Server Fleet Healer): extend pod_healer.rs + new survival_coordinator.rs in racecontrol.
- Layer 3 (External Guardian): NEW crate rc-guardian deployed to Bono VPS via pm2 (Linux target).
- OpenRouter client must be extracted to rc-common to avoid circular dependency (rc-watchdog cannot import rc-agent).
- Build order: Foundation (rc-common types) → Layer 2 Server endpoints → Layer 1 Watchdog → Layer 2 Integration → Layer 3 Guardian.

## FEATURES FINDINGS:
- Layer 1 table stakes: SHA256 validation, rollback, direct HTTP reporting, startup health poll, MAINTENANCE_MODE auto-clear.
- Layer 2 table stakes: SSH diagnostic runner, fleet-pattern detection, repair confidence gate, canary rollout, post-fix behavioral verification.
- Layer 3 table stakes: Server health polling (60s, 3-miss threshold), restart via SSH with billing safety check, WhatsApp escalation.
- Unified MMA Protocol: 5th model (thinking variant), fact-checker role, cost guard, structured taxonomy.
- Training period (30 days): top-tier models. Post-training: cheaper models based on collected data.

## PITFALLS FINDINGS:
- P1: Recovery System Fight — 5 independent healers (rc-sentry, RCWatchdog, Smart Watchdog MMA, Fleet Healer SSH, External Guardian) will conflict. Need HEAL_IN_PROGRESS sentinel with TTL.
- P2: MAINTENANCE_MODE has no timeout for MMA cycle. Watchdog must NEVER write MAINTENANCE_MODE, only read it.
- P3: Windows SYSTEM-context HTTP may fail certificate validation. Test on real pod hardware.
- P4: Rollback loop if both current and prev binaries are bad. Need rollback-state.json with depth tracking.
- P5: Split-brain between James and Bono guardians. Need GUARDIAN_ACTING coordination via comms-link.

## CONTEXT:
- 8 Windows gaming pods with sim racing hardware (wheelbases, pedals)
- Server runs on .23 (Windows), Bono VPS on Linux
- rc-agent runs in Session 1 (interactive), rc-watchdog in Session 0 (SYSTEM service)
- Existing MI v26.0 has 5-tier diagnosis, SQLite KB, gossip protocol
- All deploy uses hash-based binary naming with rc-agent-prev.exe rollback
- OpenRouter management key: sk-or-v1-5e8e94ea... (API Key: Extra Usage Back Up)
- Budget: $10/day/pod, $20/day/server
- Standing rule: Session 0 cannot launch GUI processes (Edge, games, overlays)
